channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: I've put some bags down on ORLY, what else? — 18 replies — 72.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I've put some bags down on ORLY, what else?

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-05-02 07:41 UTC

OP

I've put some bags down on ORLY, what else? New cars are abysmal and going forward for next 20 years people will only be able to afford repairing their current shitboxes. What else looks good for the long run? Oil? The earth will make oil forever, EV scam is nearing totality before collapse when oil is still cheaper and cleaner than ever for moving nations and industry.

Early replies (first 3)

62180131
>>62180126
Shit also forgot Purina, pets and related since half of millennial women have no offspring.

62180160
>>62180126
I see you've never seriously worked on cars.

protip: nobody buys from a physical parts store unless they need the part that day, and even then ORLY usually doesn't have it on hand and charges 3-10 times more than the major online competitor.

62180184
>>62180126
>>62180131
You have a child's understanding of the market, lmao.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62180197
you think these they/them faggot zoomers are gonna pick up a wrench?

62180391
>>62180160
I lived by an oreilly store once that could get any part in a couple hours. Really came in handy rebuilding a corvette. And I mean ANY PART they have in distribution. Oreilly is fuckin awesome

62180460
>>62180391
mine takes a day but they can get pretty much anything in 24 hours

I can still get it cheaper and faster off RockAuto even using next-day shipping.

Probably depends where you're located. I'm rural. We have an O'reilly in town but the next one is well over 100 miles away.

not sure if RockAuto is public, but that's probably a better bet than ORLY

62180464
>>62180391
the two things I love about O'reilly are tool rental and diagnostics. They're really good if you don't have the expensive code readers or one-time use tools.

they do get slaughtered on prices though, compared to online-only dealers.

62180477
>>62180160
Retard
>>62180184
>t. swing trader zoomie with 4 second attention span

62180482
>>62180160
you're obviously not a mechanic
if you have a commercial account you get almost everything at wholesale
i buy from them daily.
they also deliver to your shop same day for free
dont be a faggot like OP

62180510
>>62180477
>>62180482
OP is betting on people fixing their own cars.

not commercial accounts
the average dude going into ORLY pays 3 times more than ordering off rockauto minimum.

if people actually go broke and have to work on their own cars, O'reilly's doesn't stand a chance.

62180520
>>62180510
okay, listen to me, since you are being a contrarian boomer who refuses to be wrong.

regardless of OP's fucking stupid investment idea, "fixing their own cars" really means taking it to a fucking mechanic who probably uses oreillys commercially. that's like 99% of their business i swear to you, retail is nothing in comparison to them. and as people buy old cars, OP will be right, like it or not. Their stock probably wont fucking move an inch though.

and just so you know, no fucking zoomer uses rock auto, that website navigation is a filter to anybody under the age of 35. im surprised you havent posted your collection of rock auto fridge magnets yet

62180526
>>62180520
kek

you make good points, but us boomers and professional mechanics all got started because we were BROKE

and BROKE people tell each other about rockauto.

O'reilly's is great if you need a part NOW. If you can wait a day or two you can save tons of money ordering online though. Same problem every single brickandmortar retailer ever had

62180533
>>62180526
oreillys has free shipping, i can get a pair of head gaskets right now that i need on their retail website for $3 cheaper than rockauto and it'll get to my house 1 day faster

i get what you're trying to say, but unless you're commercial, most parts suppliers are charging the exact same price for just about everything. there's little margin here. sometimes ebay and amazon will even be better than both.

62180538
>>62180533
yep, depends on the part, how fast you need it, and where you're located

but if you're not in a hurry, rockauto will generally beat oreilly's even after shipping. For a lot of high-end parts it will beat oreilly's even with next-day shipping.

I check them both. I compare prices. I buy off rockauto 99% of the time because it's cheaper even after shipping. It's just slower.

I'm not trying to knock ORLY. Seriously when you need a part right now they're the best bet.

I just worry they won't survive the internets. Presumably they're living off commercial sales, but I don't know if that's enough.

62180543
>>62180538
rock auto is more complete, and also survives off of commercial sales. i personally havent bothered because they are a bitch to set it up and it doesnt affect my bottom line as much as you'd think.
they'll be fine. oreillys will stand as long as brick and mortars are around. i worry more about the nwo trying to take away private vehicle ownership or just choke us out with rising fuel costs.

62180550
>>62180543
the recent John Deer right-to-repair lawsuit was promising

and it's a fact that sometimes people need parts immediately and are willing to pay far more to get them fast.

I guess time will tell, but I don't share OP's confidence in brick and mortar. Even when it's a great company like ORLY. Much of the US is rural, and we mail-order everything

I like the piston captchas

62180567
>>62180550
>Much of the US is rural,
though to be fair I expect most PEOPLE in the US are urban and suburban. So most of the money isn't in rural sales.

I don't have a crystal ball, I don't know what stocks will do. But I do know O'reilly's is often in rural areas that don't use it much.

62180573
>>62180567
>O'reilly's is often in rural areas that don't use it much.
my town for example doesn't have a McD's or a KFC or Wallyworld but we have an O'reilly's that's in constant danger of going out of business. They're spread a bit thin if they're in towns that can't even support a McDonalds.

we be mail-ordering our big macs here. we definitely don't mind mail-ordering car parts.

item.title: I F U C KING SUFFER IN DENVER REEEEEEEEEE — 45 replies — 70.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
I F U C KING SUFFER IN DENVER REEEEEEEEEE

Open thread • Replies: 45 • Updated: 2026-05-02 07:12 UTC

OP

I F U C KING SUFFER IN DENVER
REEEEEEEEEE

Early replies (first 3)

62173936
>>62173926
Trust the plan goy

62173941
>>62173926
gas in california is over $6 right now, you have it easy all things considered

62174058
>>62173926
Its 9.35 in Germany if you calculate it in your weird metric. But the median net salary is only 40k dollar for ful time work.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62174944
>>62174544
>only $4
It's only $3.39 though. Not sure what you're griping about, this is a literal nothingburger.
>passed up 4.17 yesterday
Lol. You voted for those gasoline taxes. You voted for those environmental regulations. You voted to shut down those fracking rigs. You voted against those pipelines. You voted to close those refineries. You did this to yourself. Now you get to lay back and enjoy the fruits of your labors. So quit your bitching, you little faggot.

62174951
>>62174944
What part of Texas is in Indiana, buddy?

62174963
You guys are almost paying California prices. It's 5.09 here right now.

62174977
>>62174944
>texas
creo tu eres un joto.

62174998
>>62173926
I voted for this

62175538
>>62174118
Yes it's very vibrant and uhm dark
>>62174126
Well not me

62175544
this is just the beginning, and this is why the current stock valuation is tarded. just wait

62175608
>>62173926
>Dinosaur in logo
"Fossil fuels"(LOL) Yes, goy, thissss oil is preciousssss

62175657
>>62174058
It's 10.33 in Israel.

62175885
>>62174944
All this effort for the most retarded post I’ve seen this week. Bravo

62175898
Good.
Let the wealth gap grow.

62176176
>>62174951
>What part of Texas is in hell, buddy?
None of it. Texas is God's country. Also, which part of
>You did this to yourself
did you fail to understand?

62176178
>>62176176
tejas is a brown shithole.

62176179
>>62173926
gas was $3.50 and now its $4.50 and thats the reason why your life is falling apart

62176310
>>62176179
yes

62176338
I know who you are OP

62176370
>>62173926
The stock market is at ATH. You lost, tranny

62176398
I really dont know why this is so bad to you. My monthly gas bill went up by like $40. Are you guys really that strapped every month that you cant afford a $40 increase?

62176486
>>62173926
Only trannies and mexicans live in Denver.

62176488
>>62176176
Good god, Texas needs to fix their education system

62176510
Commiefornians flooded your state right before Obummer ran for office, and now you're hurting from it.
> inb4 "TRUMP CAUSED THIS"
Well no shit, point I'm making here is the blue states have even higher gas prices compared to others thanks to outrageous environmentalist laws from the commie mofos.

62176513
>>62173926
It just hit $4 in New Hampshire. Nobody even cares that I am a man trying on woman's clothing in the fitting room anymore. They all sense the same thing.

62176514
>>62173926
Cheap

62176819
>>62174083
> germany makes 0 oil
> germany imports all of its oil and energy
> germany pays out the ass
many such cases

62176841
>>62173926
Everyone at work is complaining about gas. I said "what's the problem?". They all looked at me weird.

Not my problem you losers drive Ford F-350s with $700/mo payments and work is 45 minutes from your house.

62177237
>>62176841
I drive an old camry

62177310
>>62173926
DAMMIT NOW I NEED MORE THAN TREE FIDDY

62178780
>Denver
Lafayette Costco Chads stay winning

62178995
>>62173926
Its $5.20 here faggot

62180531
You didn't buy a hybrid? Thought we all did that?

item.title: $Trump Sisters, When Do We PUMP?? — 14 replies — 56.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
$Trump Sisters, When Do We PUMP??

Open thread • Replies: 14 • Updated: 2026-05-02 07:49 UTC

OP

I bought at $56.
I know $500 is possible.

Early replies (first 3)

62179895
1500 is programmed by 2027
TTP

62179899
>>62179889
Some guy I know had his entire portfolio take a 90% haircut after trump coin launched, he then aped the remaining money into trump coin at the top. Shell of a man nowadays

62180002
>>62179899
I got railed pretty hard in late 2023 and haven't touched crypto since. I have never been happier.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62180027
There's a mineable trump coin (merge mining with litecoin btw) and I thought it was going to die with Trump's reputation because of the Iran war but somehow the price keeps pumping. Still only a 400k fully diluted market cap

62180063
it's going to zero. they will blame congress to deflect from themselves

62180347
>>62179889
589$ tumor weeks blody!

62180350
>>62179889
trust the plan, we go to the moon in 14 days

62180351
kek

62180430
>>62179889
>79889▶>>62179899 >>62180347 >>62180350
>I bought at $56.
$2000 by the end of my term. Thank you for your attention to this matter!

- Donald J. Trump

62180432
>>62180430
based trump

62180471
>>62179889
don't you guys do buff trumps in here?

62180508
kek i made like 50k off this the day it went to 100,
sucks to be you baggies

62180545
>>62180508
Twas never 100$, you had a dream.

62180582
>>62180545
you're fucking retarded, it tapped 100 dollars. i sold at 65.

item.title: What can I do — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
What can I do

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-05-02 07:50 UTC

OP

I just turned 18 last week I have no job no career no college no car just graduated high school only have 3k saved. What should I do to continue living and make my way as an adult? I feel useless and incapable. I can walk 20 minutes to some places for work but it’s all fast food and they won’t even reply to my applications and I have absolutely nothing to put on resumes but I’m thinking about lying for minor achievements until I can get my first job. I’m NGMI right? What have you done to get up and become an adult in the world? Must I go to college? I live at home and have no government assistance. Also despite being a female anon I’m too ugly for sex work so no suggestions towards that thanks help me out finance anons, finally I don’t have a license but I can work towards that easily. In America.

Early replies (first 3)

62180503
>>62180499
>female
sell feet pics

62180511
>>62180499
go to hvac school

62180517
Pray.
>>>/bant/24269377

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62180519
>>62180499
22april25: 30$
toDAY: 1187$
3000>118,700

62180524
>>62180499
BUY nvax.
150 is sure.
hope 300

62180529
>>62180499
You must have a shitty home life if your parents aren't encouraging you to go to college. What you really need is a mentor (not me) to guide you towards making the best decisions at your baby age of 18.

62180561
>>62180499
tits

item.title: The difference between us and you goycattle is that our great grandfathers invested, our grandfathers invested, and our… — 8 replies — 11.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
The difference between us and you goycattle is that our great grandfathers invested, our grandfathers invested, and our…

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-05-02 07:08 UTC

OP

The difference between us and you goycattle is that our great grandfathers invested, our grandfathers invested, and our fathers invested, so we started life with a century of compounding behind us. Meanwhile, YOUR great grandfather, grandfather, and father all drank, smoked, and gambled away their money like the goycattle they are.

Early replies (first 3)

62180472
>>62180448
Bruh.

62180481
>>62180448
there's no such thing as goyim, goycattle, NPCs, etc
becoming a nihilistic jew is not the way to fight the jews my nigga

62180483
i have a feeling nobody is interested in that though
few people are actually interested in living in a jew-free society, they're more interested in a carnival they never realized they signed up for
happens with every generation, i guess?
i just can't stand it sometimes

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62180484
the fact you'd bring up smoking as a negative & then invoke smoking pepe supports what i just said, it's all just entertainment to you

62180486
>>62180483
what's there to living? blurry toil that fills the space between carnivals

62180490
>>62180486
it ain't supposed to be cold & blurry like that

world is unnaturally cold right now
as much as the meme "we live in a society" is repeated, we actually DON'T
we live in a big economy
economy & society are at odds with each other when each taken to their conclusions
that's why the world is so cold, the big economy

62180497
but the point is that shit is unnatural, it's not inherent to this world
when you ask what there is to living, i wish i knew what to say
but we all live in the same big roblox server with one another, you can really make anything you want of it
there are plenty of ways to live which you may not be aware of, and i wish you would try to change your life in drastic ways before giving up with an outlook like that

there are always very quiet groups of people being un-self-aware & living in warmth with each other & doing things they find meaningful
i wish i could think of examples, it probably just sounds like trash

sorry anon i wish i knew

62180523
>>62180490
it's not necessarily cold, normal life is largely uninteresting and solved, outside of carnivals or watching lesser beings, like children, grow and do all sorts of dumb and memorable things
there's nothing very bad about it, it's just an observation, and, after all, that's why we have invented carnivals and smoking and all kinds of distractions
we could have lived in huts huddled together, writing and retelling cozy stories, and it'd probably be interesting for a generation or 20, but it sounds like a variety of carnival that you underexperienced and want now, but even that someday will naturally get bland and a certain craving will push you to look for something else, and hopefully it will not be something terminal like gambling
I guess that's human

item.title: Is it worse to be a wagie or a NEET? — 179 replies — 48.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Is it worse to be a wagie or a NEET?

Open thread • Replies: 179 • Updated: 2026-05-02 04:08 UTC

OP

Is it worse to be a wagie or a NEET?

Early replies (first 3)

62158601
>>62158557
Neet
Wagie can get some money to go out and have fun, and socialize.
might not be your cup of tea, until you making out with a 8/10 girl who you just said hi to, 2 minutes ago

62158623
>>62158601
>might not be your cup of tea, until you making out with a 8/10 girl who you just said hi to, 2 minutes ago

while waging at walmart is impossible

62158626
>>62158557
I prefer to be a neet, but ofc neither situation is perfect. Now if i was getting pussy then we'd be talking

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62169232
>>62159483
better than she having you wait while fucking every other dude on a first date

62169261
>>62158666
Skill issue, Satan. There's much fun to be had for free.

62169348
>>62167534
>I only have one perspective so my perspective is right
Great now I know not to take your opinion seriously.

62169412
>>62158557
It's worse to be a wagie, obviously. Why would anyone ever think being NEET is worse? The whole point of working is to save up enough money to be NEET someday.

62169467
>>62169412
Yes and waging gets you more money and expedites that process so you can eventually neet. If you go neet mode with no money you're just suffering and can't truly enjoy life, and the "enjoyment" you have is just consuming media which makes you as much of a goy as a wagie.

62169540
>>62159267
Kierkegaard was based Dane, like Schacht.

62170345
>>62158557
depends on your situation. neet is better when you can actually afford it. if you need money then having a job is better no matter how soul crushing it is most of the time.

62170481
>>62159382
At least you know what checked actually means unlike that other dude.
But honestly man, why would my view be goyish? I am just saying biology will motivate you in life aka a hot woman
>>62169232
Fuck yeah dude. A hot woman keep saying I love you while pulling your hair, moaning and can’t stop herself from kissing you? Yeah I don’t care what she did with who before at that moment
>>62169261
Bro I had a one full terabyte of Skyrim and it’s mod enable on my shit laptop and was playing it without issue, so I have no trouble going around my laptop limitation. I could entertain myself with two absolutly simple games like xcom piretez or dominion 6 (both that I loved) then when I got bored I would used chatgpt to make assets and modded the game to my taste myself and started over, so no, I know how to not get bored either.
Problem is that a shit laptop is just not able to handle all the calculation of ck3 or stellaris (thanks to paradox shit programming) and total war had to be played on low settings.
I don’t like that shit. I don’t wanna be limited like this. Why would I limit myself to some fake and not my favorite, while I can go work, make some money, and get a pc that can handle ALL MY GAMES?
>>62169467
Yep. A smart wagie is a free man.
my problem with being a neet was always lack of safety net, but a wagie has that and he can get out of rat race faster.
>>62169412
Sure, we all love to be neet. But being a retired YOUNG neet, with money and your own car and place, is hard fucking work. (Or luck)
You can either sit on your ass and wish to be like that, have a good luck in lotto or stocks or crypto or work your ass and wage. In the end, the wagie gonna win, because he got more money to dump into stocks and crypto while a neet doesnt.
And god, I fucking WISHED I had my own place right now dude.

62170488
>>62170481
It’s still me, I don’t know why my id keep changing, probably cause I am a phonefag posting at work

62170497
Either has their pros and cons. Waging can get enraging and demoralizing but is a wonderful source of social, schedule, productivity, and income. Neeting has freedom and self-expression but can get so isolating and repetitive that it can make you break down mentally entirely over time.

Neeting and waging are two sides of the same coin. Balance in all things and you will find peace.

62171998
>>62158557
NEETs have dignity.

62175198
>>62158601
Skill issue. I'm working on become turboneet through three sources and will clear 93-95k when they are approved.

62175347
>>62170481
>>62170345
Almost everyone that owns a house in a top tier city came from well off families. I don’t think the majority truly realize how important family help is and how big the wealth gap is.
Good houses in good areas are truly unaffordable for normal or people in the bottom 95%.

62175361
>>62170497
> Either has their pros and cons. Waging can get enraging and demoralizing but is a wonderful source of social, schedule, productivity, and income. Neeting has freedom and self-expression but can get so isolating and repetitive that it can make you break down mentally entirely over time.
There is nothing productive about waking up at 6am everyday. Neet is a choice but waging isn’t. I think this is what no one wants to say. No one working would choose to work if they didn’t have to work.

62175389
>>62158557
Diogenes, Buddha and Jesus was Neets
nobody remembers the trillions of wagies
being a wagie is worser than being a neet

62175537
>>62167410
the real best of both worlds is WFH wagie
>forced into a regular wake up schedule that isn’t obscenely early or late
>no commute
>less oversight allows you to better enjoy the downtime inherent to any job, without giving you so much free time as to burn out media quickly
>access to your own kitchen during breaks and all the wagie bucks to buy good ingredients
>once again no commute, allowing you to instantly switch to leisure the moment the workday is done

62176675
>>62168012
https://soul-zone.org/

62177246
>>62158601
40 years ago maybe

62177573
>>62168700
>when a girl you just saw is now CRAZY into you, to the point of saying she loved you, keep trying to makeout with you, is just all over you
pointless to think about because it has never happened and will never happen

62177728
neets arent masculine and no one respects them

62179485
At least neets get to sleep.

62179529
neets because there's nothing like realizing you wasted your whole damn life and also you're a lazy piece of shit that should've just killed yourself

62179583
>>62179529
i genuinely don't know whether you're talking about wagies or neets

62179651
>>62168700
> social media lied to me
> i didn’t participate in it
> mfw
I’m not saying you’re choosing wrong, not everyone is suited to NEET life and the world needs productive people.
But maybe just have some faith in yourself that you were making your own bad choices and then turned it around when you found motivation.

62179923
After having neet'maxxd and wage cage'd and finally landed at a job I actually don't mind that pays very well the last is infinitely preferable to both of the former, but I'd put NEET above any true wagie job. I see some people act like a mundane office job is wagie work and I just assume they've never had to work in a truly dead end hellscape position. There are some truly soul-crushing jobs out there.

62179934
>>62165202
Being able to show up at the same time every day is a skill. Dealing with people is a skill.

62179952
>>62166566
It's ok anon. You picked yourself up. I fucked off in my 20s. I wasn't a neet but I have nothing to show for it. Didn't save any money. Thankfully I did enough to get into a good law school and I just graduated at 33. But it does suck being years behind people who are younger than me and making partner. However you have years and years of career ahead of you. Don't look back.

62179958
>>62166748
People who had parents who can leave an inheritance worth anything are generally successful. You're a crab.

62180244
>>62179934
Seriously. Going to work everyday day is a skill. So tempting to just take a day off.

62180261
>>62177246
>>62177573
Ok guys
I gonna go tonight again.
and next day.
If I didn’t end up making out with a hot a girl like last time, I will get back here and tell you it might have been a fluke
>>62179651
I said that because tons of guys are also getting blasted by social media and just gave up

item.title: Woman JP Morgan executive Rapes and turns Male Employee into sex slave — 142 replies — 38.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Woman JP Morgan executive Rapes and turns Male Employee into sex slave

Open thread • Replies: 142 • Updated: 2026-05-02 04:09 UTC

OP

https://x.com/dailymail/status/2049618941043949691?s=46

A white woman JP Morgan Executive (mega international bank) drugged and raped a married man before taking pictures of him to use as blackmail.

She would force him to take drugs like viagra so she could fuck him longer. She started buying him gifts which as sex toys including anal ones which she forced him to use and got him a position where she had direct control over him and he acted as her assistant. She also pegged him.

He was crying over the situation and talking to authorities who didn’t believe him.

She was caught when police investigated her for ties to Trump and Epstein with her blackmailing the employee being unclassified.

Early replies (first 3)

62172413
>>62172412

62172414
>>62172413
kek

62172420
>>62172413
post her tits are they big and nice?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62176111
giwtwm...

62176126
>>62172413
so this took place at his house? where was his wife?

62176236
Bros...i need an albanian wife who sits on my face nightly

62176255
>>62175043
she's in a male-dominated space because she acts like a man

62176674
APOLOGIZE

62176678
jeets unironically think life is like a porno

62176680
>>62172412
Shoulda punched her, killed her, but "that's not civilized" so the frustrated man lost. This is a silly new story.

62176682
>>62175811
the second I saw his photo I knew it was fake

62176704
>>62176682
The moment I read that at least his feet don't smell like curry or some other comment in the allegations, there was no way.

62176816
Why the fuck did the guy report his fantasies as reality? Did he think he could speak them into existence? Lmfaooo holy shit. Dudes about to lose his job and his wife when it comes out he fabricated everything

62176825
>>62176816
turns out he doesnt have a wife either

62176949
For anyone checking in, the story was fake made up by a jeet who probably got fired. Internal investigation by JP morgan showed no evidence of anything happening within the buildings where this woman worked. She didn't even know him.

62176953
>>62172412
Based

62176970
>>62176949
She did know him and he tried to do an internal complaint but then refused to actually answer any questions when the company investigated LMAO

62176973
>>62175043
I use it all the time and now I’m sad that I can’t go to warosu and search “fish head” and see exclusively my posts now

62176986
>>62176674
Smoke show

62176988
>>62176674
Look at those assets
No wonder she works at a bank

62176994
>>62175043
she's albanian not white

62176996

item.title: >working overtime and days off >fell into the next tax bracket >barely make anything despite working more HAHAHAHAHAHA I… — 17 replies — 18.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>working overtime and days off >fell into the next tax bracket >barely make anything despite working more HAHAHAHAHAHA I…

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-05-02 06:53 UTC

OP

>working overtime and days off
>fell into the next tax bracket
>barely make anything despite working more
HAHAHAHAHAHA
I AM JUST A FUCKING GOYIM
I now respect criminals and will begin stealing, I mean borrowing..

Early replies (first 3)

62179831
>>62179750
>working overtime and days off
>fell into the next tax bracket
>barely make anything despite working more
It doesn't work like that.

62179839
>>62179750
Stop working as much then?

62179900
>>62179750
You don’t understand how tax brackets work

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62179904
>>62179831
I think what OP means, which I also felt when moving from one bracket to another, is that the government immediately fucks you hard with taxes in ways you had no cclue would hit you. And makes you realize how poor you are because even if you get lucky and make 10k a month, they'll fuck you hard on everything over a certain point while Jew Man in the white house is making billions from illegally insider trading.

Actually even better- Surgeons make 800k a year. The system is designed to keep you poor and elevate a handful of scammerres and psychos to richville, what's even better is they'll try to obfuscate the good careers and gatekeep. You literally can't work, educate yourself, do business etc. to riches because they will try to fuck you.

62180040
This is absolutely fucking not how taxes work I the US. (You) are a neet who lives in a moldy basement and only derive meaning from making demoralization threads. Do you also post bbc on /gif/?

62180042
>>62179904
>the government immediately fucks you hard with taxes
It doesn't work like that at all. What sort of hell hole do you live in?
>I also felt when moving from one bracket to another
No, you didn't. Also, this is how we know that not only have you never moved from one bracket to another, but you have never held a job in a first world country.

62180054
>>62180042
It does work like that, please get a real job and sotp giving bad info online you pathetic neet loser.

62180069
>>62179831
Yes it does. It's called average hourly take-home pay which can absolutely be lower if your salary increases but your work hours and taxable income also increases.

62180086
>>62180042
Trump's 2025 tax bracket min wage law was to counter this but many do not care and just take it away still.
The only way ahead is theft and violence.

62180132
>>62180086
> Many do not care and take it away still
What are you talking about?
Change your deductions or something, retards

62180181
>>62179750
I would sexo if you know what i mean bro's

62180186
>>62180069
Correct me if I'm wrong but doesn't only the new portion of your income get taxed higher. So the other portion of the income gets taxed the way it's always been, therefore you'll be earning more but maybe not on a linear scale.

62180251
I can't tell who's trolling who
In case there are legitimately tax illiterate people here, your annual income is split into brackets that are taxed at a progressively higher rate.

Your first 12K @ 10%
Your next 38K @ 12%

62180276
>>62179831
It does, making 50% more for 100% more work is a raw deal.

62180354
>>62179750
>posts beautiful Japanese girl
>posts niggerishly barbaric extremely un-Japanese nigger dirt-brown shitskinned stinky asshole unclean subhuman vermin brown ugly third-world type post

The duality of 4chan

62180385

> lose 20% of my previous salary
> but I enter previous tax bracket
> and finally have the time to wok on the house myself instead of having to pay others to do so
I earn almost as much as I did previously, biggest issue is getting a promotion when not working full time

62180491
>>62180181
yeah me too

item.title: The US national debt is now bigger than the entire US economy Today's debt milestone is not borne from war mobilization. — 28 replies — 17.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
The US national debt is now bigger than the entire US economy Today's debt milestone is not borne from war mobilization.

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-05-02 06:15 UTC

OP

The US national debt is now bigger than the entire US economy
Today's debt milestone is not borne from war mobilization. It is the accumulated result of tax cuts without matching reductions in spending, an aging population drawing more heavily on Social Security and Medicare, and years of political leadership on both sides of the aisle that has consistently chosen short-term popularity over long-term solvency.
You fags are cooked (including me)

Early replies (first 3)

62179629
Doesn’t matter, because no one can collect from the US Govt

Refute this without sounding mad

62179630
>>62179622
breaking news: voters want stuff and dont want to pay for it

62179853
>>62179622
AI will fix this

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62179912
>>62179622
Interest and entitlements is almost 100% of revenue what do you cut thay woukd be meaningful exactly, I was in the camp that thought rates should never have been raised as high and definitely should have been cut it being the biggest inflation driver

62179921
>>62179622
It was designed that. It's a feature.

62179928
>>62179622
Come and take the debt, you will quickly find out why nobody has ever invaded the United States. Imagine 300 million Americans using guerilla warfare tactics, you would have to fight for every inch of this country. We will never pay that bullshit imaginary money back and there is genuinely nothing you can fucking do about it and this goes to every europoor trash on this website

62179957
>>62179629
china might beable2 doe
but then i don't really know if that's how national debt works in general, ur post seems off-topic

62179961
>>62179928

i don't understand enough about the world to pick which one
but i wish this board were better

62179968
wish i understood this shit
wish i understood the federal reserve
wish i understood glowies & moloch cults

62179970
>>62179622
I'm a goldminer chad. I'm going to make an obnoxious amount of money when this war is over.

Feels good.


Later losers.

62180005
>>62179970

62180031

item.title: I CANT STAND WAGING HOW THE FUCK DO PEOPLE DO THIS FOR 30+ YEARS???? — 54 replies — 10.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
I CANT STAND WAGING HOW THE FUCK DO PEOPLE DO THIS FOR 30+ YEARS????

Open thread • Replies: 54 • Updated: 2026-05-02 02:28 UTC

OP

I CANT STAND WAGING HOW THE FUCK DO PEOPLE DO THIS FOR 30+ YEARS????

Early replies (first 3)

62165377
>>62165360
Become an overnight security guard with a site in a rich area that’s internal (you’re inside a building).

The golden goose is MAG7 tech companies. I’ve literally and unironically done security for google. Max comfy

62165379
>>62165360
I do. I work like ~5 hours a day and play on my phone while eating a ton of corporate junk food and hitting on the co-workers for the rest. Must be a skill issue.

62165445
>>62165360
E Z ly
U just week

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62166949
>>62165360
Idk. My job is really good and I’ve been doing it 15 years, the only job I’ve ever been able to do this long, and it’s still hell.

62166970
>>62166949
Same, my job is good for what it is. I've only been in the workforce for 5 years and I think I'm going to kill myself.

62167031
>>62165377
You’re essentially lower class.
>>62165806
When you realize 99% of people are born to work.

62168374
>>62166493
I fear that is already happening to me :(

62168766
>>62166949
>>62166970
Sounds like no matter what you did, you’d still bitch about everything.

62168822
my job is ok, but it's annoying to have to come in when I could do it all remotely, too bad I can't quit and neet but I like my lakehouse and boat too much to give them up.

62168835
Idk, I work 18 hours a week as a teacher for 2000€ and it's already too much work, how do you guys do 40 hours a week I will never understand

62171668
>>62165360
>HOW THE FUCK DO PEOPLE DO THIS FOR 30+ YEARS????
1) they have low standards
2) they'r NPCs just following orders
3) they hate every minute of it but are too scared to end themselves
4) they hate every minute of it and use that hatred to keep going
5) they hate every minute of it but are too scared to take risks

62171714
>>62165830
And how will these cameras and locks stop criminals?
Human presence alone deters 90% of potential theft.
>t. zoom zooms and niggers see me and v line the other way

62172531
>>62165360
The trick is you don't live long enough to ever really finish. it's a shell game where you believe that at the end there is something better waiting for you when really if you didn't have it to begin with you were never going to have it.

62172580
>>62165377
>I’ve literally and unironically done security for google.

How would you do it figuratively and ironically?

62172962
if you hate your shit do something else. people do it 30 years cause they dont hate it enough to stop

62173033
>>62165360
Find a good job.
I am slaving for shareholders but we have fun and the pay is not bad.

62173103
>>62165360
seriously, become black, get money from gob, steal and rape white goys and be recognized as an eternal victim that deserves everyone's favors and sympathy

62173916
>>62165647
I would rope if I have to work in an open plan office like that again.
For me, it's full WFH now or nothing.

62173930
>>62168835
this also jelly about the 18h. currently at 32 and that shit is borderline doable. dont know how I was able to do 40 the first few years. retards are basically throwing their life away for 15-20$ an hour. you cant make this shit up and at the end of the month their bank account still ends up being at 0 lol

62174044
>>62168835
I am a farmer and I choose how much I work most of the year. I have also worked in factories so I know what a 40 hour week is like. After I work 6 hours in a day I really want to be done and think about offing myself. If I do no work at all in a day I feel useless and think about offing myself. I think 3-4 hours of work per day is the sweet spot. It is just enough to feel like I accomplished something but not too much that I hate my life.

62174065
>>62165360
It could be worse. You could be a professional.

I'm in litigation hell.

62174236
>>62165360
I'm in the process of becoming Uberneet
>100% VA rated
>Medical retirement from fed job
Can't stand it anymore. Fuck them all

62174313
it's not sustainable, you gain weight, sleep deprivation in the norm etc.

even if you make it retirement age of 68yo, you gonna have a heart attack year after retiring because that 35 years of waging taking toll on body

you need to have a remote job at minimum. but preferable something like trading or playing poker in Pattaya isntead of waging ofcourse is the optimal. me have never had a proper job after some summer jobs, it was horrendous counting the minutes and watching clock to hit 4pm to get home

62176245
>>62165360
amass 200k and buy yourself 10ish years in Thailand to figure it out

62176325
>>62165360
In the past?
Parental duties and drugs.

62177541
>>62165360
I have a job where 50% of the time I'm relaxing and shitposting and even I can't stand it lmao

Neet is king

62177592
>>62165377
Another security guard here, don't take this advice. I used to do security overnight in malls at luxury retail stores (whenever they had any renovations or if the door was broken or something). It messes up your sleep schedule, it's a minimum wage job with no career advancement opportunities and If they can easily replace you for a job you are not worth anything. Get a blue-collar job or go to college and get an office job, it is a million times better than being a minimum-wage cuck.

62177734
>>62165360
What else are you going to do for 30+ years fag. Nothing? Might as well kill yourself now. Save yourself years of monotony

62179669
>>62173916
I don't wage anymore but holy fucking shit man these offices, no work can be done there.
After covid they all had the smart idea to downsize buildings so all meeting rooms were booked 24/7, I used to be in these all day long to work on stuff and have calls.
After that I decided to go full home office + customer, fuck this shit. Whoever thought that was a good idea is completely fucking retarded.

62179692
>>62177734
> monotony
You mean wage slaving?

62179698
>>62177592
Yeah, decent security jobs are hard to find and night shift work isn’t for everyone. I don’t mind it but i knew a guy who basically broke down on shift because he couldn’t adjust and hadn’t slept in 6wks.
Felt bad for him but he didn’t have to fail so hard he left in the middle of shift. That was lame

62179714
>>62177541
If I’m going to be at a job, i want to be busy. Too much downtime at an office pisses me off, like i may as well be at home.
So now i wfh and can enjoy when i have down time instead of seething, and i genuinely get more work done. Like if i think of something at 10 pm, I’m just going get on my computer and do it instead of waiting and then forgetting by morning too

62180049
>>62165360
Do you have any idea how satisfying it is to see the airplane I just built take off?
Shit never gets old.
14 years later, still good feels every time.
And now it's a $130,000 a year job.
Even better

ka

item.title: Clarity Act Version goes Public — 26 replies — 8.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Clarity Act Version goes Public

Open thread • Replies: 26 • Updated: 2026-05-02 04:36 UTC

OP

Coinbase kowtowed.

> The compromise from Sens. Tillis (R) and Alsobrooks (D) bans rewards "economically or functionally equivalent" to deposit interest. Activity-based rewards tied to payments, transfers, and real platform usage stay protected. The structure closes affiliate workarounds by design.

> Coinbase Chief Policy Officer Faryar Shirzad (@faryarshirzad) framed the outcome as a win on what mattered: "the ability for Americans to earn rewards, based on real usage of crypto platforms and networks." Banks got tighter restrictions on yield. Crypto kept the activity rails open.

> The final text emerged from months of negotiation between the White House, Treasury, Senate Banking GOP, Tillis, and Alsobrooks. Senate Banking Committee markup is targeted for mid-May, lining up with Sen. Scott's "in the red zone" comments yesterday.

> Remaining issues to resolve: DeFi provisions, ethics language for executive branch officials, and reconciliation with the House-passed version.

Early replies (first 3)

62179413
>>62179409
In other words, 2 more weeks bros.

62179423
>>62179409
I guess this probably benefits things like Ethereum and DeFi but it's still bullshit that banks can tell me what I'm allowed to do with my 1s and 0s.

62179430
>>62179413
>mid-May
unironically

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62179439
>>62179423
Is AMM/LP not considered 'real platform usage' within:
>Activity-based rewards tied to payments, transfers, and real platform usage stay protected.
?

62179454
>>62179439
It is considered real platform usage. The hold up was because banks wanted to block Coinbase from acting like a bank where you hold crypto and get EZ APY for keeping it on their platform. The banks won.

62179609
>>62179454
Is there a good way to just get passive yield using treasuries? I searched fidelity and there were a bajillion order book. I like when cb had USDC yield, deposit -> receive.

62179624
>>62179409
Bump. So when does this pass?

62179641
>>62179624
they're now aiming for june. kek

62179647
aka chainlink staking 1.0 incoming

62179765
>>62179647
This has literally nothing to do with Chainlink. If v1 was actually viable they would have done it already. Eth released staking while under the iron fist of Gensler and nothing happened to them from a regulatory standpoint. Its just not happenin bro

62179781
>>62179765
eth staking was released to cuck gpu mining nothing more nothing less, its essentially the same as link staking 0.1

real staking however, is fully cleared for launch when this clarity act goes through

62179842
>>62179641
It’s over

62179848
>>62179781
The SEC and CFTC have both already classified the link token as a digital commodity. What is the Clarity Act going to do that hasn't already been done?

62179852
>>62179423
you can still do whatever you want it just won't be funded by institutions

62179860
>>62179765
Anon, Chainlink's staking is for the banks. They are the ones in most need of the security staking offers.

62179883
>>62179860
>>62179781
>staking is ready
please describe a trustless mechanism for proving and slashing an invalid oracle report. for example, proving to the Ethereum staking module that a CCIP message between Solana and Base L2 was not delivered or was fraudulent. I'll save you the time and tell you that trustlessness is impossible in this case. So it must come down to a vote. In which case the voting must be done by trusted parties. Where is staking necessary in this process?

62179916
>>62179883
you're a bitch

62180038
>>62179409
>Passive rewards banned.
Uh, doesn't that include staking?

62180044
>>62180038
depends. if the rewards are from "activity" then it's fine. validator rewards for example, which covers most staking. I think a grey area is liquidity farming, which is mostly dead these days, but prominent in solidity-style dexes like aerodrome. but there the dex is voting explicitly to pay liquidity providers, and the dex gets paid by transaction fees, so I think it's still gucci.

62180127
>>62179883
/thread
Clarity act bullish for everything except worthless stink

62180138
polymarket odds have just skyrocketed

62180147
>>62179883
>I'll save you the time and tell you that trustlessness is impossible in this case
true trustless-ness is impossible at any level
trust-minimized systems are possible if you make discrete validations that can be presented and extracted as form of slashing
for orcales, that is easier said than done: you're trying to prove a data point was correct and the other wasn't, in a way that cannot be defrauded

62180176
>>62179409
This btfo’s the exchanges, everyone keeps their stables on exchanges for safe and decent APR, when that’s no longer the case there will be a mass exodus of dollars from exchanges and hyper liquid will explode

62180191
>>62179883
>So it must come down to a vote. In which case the voting must be done by trusted parties. Where is staking necessary in this process?

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oXVgYty_UdA

You're describing Quadratic staking actually. Where if there is a disagreement on the oracle report, the first honest node to report to a higher tier of "trusted nodes" gets the collateral of all the incorrect/malicious nodes if its proven that node is correct while the malicious nodes are incorrect.

To expand on the video; if there are 10 nodes, each staking $1M in a DON, you generally want the consensus to sway in your favor; 6/10 saying your malicious Yes instead of No for some Polymarket trade. But the staking mechanism here is that the first node to report the malicious node wins the collateral of the malicious nodes. So those 4 honest nodes would race to a higher tier of nodes to report the other 6 to get their collateral worth $6M. Thus Juels there is implying if 1 node is honest and the rest are malicious, he'd get $9M if he reports them all. So you'd have to bribe all 10 nodes, 10*$1M for each of them; so 100*$1M in total.

So to answer your question, staking is involved to incentivize nodes with no reputation to rat each other out to other trusted nodes. On top of that, it also allows NEET nodes, nodes with no reputation behind them, to join on mainnet and participate in DONs as banks use them to play with their NFTs.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Z6ejrz0XvQc

62180278
>activity based rewards
Chainlink stays winning.

62180309
>>62179409
Saaars shainlaink! Basterd!
Thos board kek

item.title: MrBeast says anyone can get a million subs by filming themselves walking across the US and having the footage profession… — 47 replies — 7.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
MrBeast says anyone can get a million subs by filming themselves walking across the US and having the footage profession…

Open thread • Replies: 47 • Updated: 2026-05-02 01:53 UTC

OP

MrBeast says anyone can get a million subs by filming themselves walking across the US and having the footage professionally edited with a pro-quality thumbnail attached. He says people don't do this because they either don't understand how easy it is to game the YouTube algorithm or they're just lazy. He says doing anything extreme on YouTube is still low-hanging fruit for fame and money, even now. So why haven't you taken his advice?

Early replies (first 3)

62161084
Even if that were true (doubt), doing youtube is gig-work, more akin to driving for Uber eats than the entrepreneurship that youtubers think they're engaging in. You can think you're "self-employed" until you do something that Google doesn't like, and then you'll find out who your boss really is.

62161086
>>62161076
The glownigger puppet wants more people to upload surveillance for the government online? And setting yourself up to be cancelled by mob mentality and no longer having actual skills for a job and a resume gap meaning you’ll never enter the workforce again?

62161087
i manage my own portfolio and fap all day, what makes you think im gonna get up and film my jew ass talking to a bunch of goyim for the entertainment of other goyim?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62162225
>>62162184
>channels
is what ruined it, just look up what you want to see and click the most homely video

62162234
>>62161086
>resume gap = you'll never enter the workforce again
sounds like the economy's problem, call me when applying for jobs is based on competence instead of meaningless retard criteria

62162262
>>62161076
He is somewhat correct, but you have to do it right requires very expensive production team

62162269
>>62161076
you also have to not be able to grow a beard above your neck

62162303
>>62161076
how do you make the thumbnail then?
I make extremely low effort shorts and get thousands of views everytime

62162786
>>62161076
Can any terminally online brown zoomer please explain to the rest of us normie chads who this guy is?

62162805
>>62161076
>So why haven't you taken his advice?
why haven't you, faggot? kys

62163322
>>62162173
I think he's more getting at an idea more than specifically advocating people walk across the US. Remember he got famous for saying "Logan Paul" 500,000 times or something i forget the amount, he did a lot of endurance challenges like that which got him famous. I think he's more saying people do something ridiculous and hard and memeable and film it but it cant be easy because then there's no skin in the game its gotta actually be hard.

62164259
>>62162212
Is this Mrbeast? Who the fuck like genuinely what fucking retarded goycattle will watch a video just because he has thumbnail like Mrbeast? Like seriously? They are a fucking meme and odd. Everyone makes fun of them not praise them like it's some sort of magic that will help your videos. Mrbeast is a clown and his goycattle fans are sub 60 IQ

62164413
this jewish white nigger slave is an industry plant, i'm not trusting a single word coming out of its mouth

62164430
>>62161076
>MrBeast says anyone can get a million subs by filming themselves walking across the US
Fuck it, call me Sam Porter.

62168164
>>62161076
>can get a million subs
This translates to how much in real currency?

62170693
>>62161084
you can say this about anything

62170796
>>62161122
This, so much this...

62171081
>>62168164
$60k to $1M a year depending on your output.

62171130
>>62161076
some buddhist monks did that and got ran over almost immediately

62171132
>>62162786
He's a 27yo YouTuber worth billions who clawed his way out of poverty just by grinding YouTube for five years, from 2012 to 2017. He leveraged his fame to build a burger company and a hugely successful candy company, and he plans to expand to video games and other industries.

62173729
>>62171132
He actually has more debt than money he claims

62173789
I just saw a black dude getting run over doing this lol. The car hit the breaks but was rear ended and that pushed him into the black guy. Am unsure if he died.

62176468
>>62161076
it would require talking and interacting which I wont do if im walking..

62176498
I tried YT for awhile. 6 channels. What I learned is you need good content for starters. But the other half of it is abso-fucking-lutely luck. The al-gore-rythem might decide to help you out on day 2, or month 2. Then, for no apparent reason, it will say fuck you and you stop getting views. Basically I threw in the rag because an automated system fucked over one channel, and due to YT's "circumvention policy" all my other channels got nuked. My Appeals were initially instantly rejected....fast forward a few months and magically one by one they started accepted appeals after I made the case that an automated system flagged my content as "hate speech" - despite said video having ZERO talking.

62176517
>trust me dude you can make a million bucks just FILMING YOURSELF
>there is no algorithm
Also I'm gonna say it- Mr. Beast claims to be 24 but he looks like he's 39. Like. He's fake. He's not a real person. He's a paid fucking actor paid to say and promote bullshit while pretending to be more than a decade younger. Fuck him.

62176555
>>62161076
>Bro just film yourself doing extreme acts and have a professional editor and photographer do the thumbnail while your CIA parents fund your endeavors.

62176565
>>62161076
nobody cares

62176570
>>62161076
I bet this could work if you filmed the whole thing and broke it into many parts with the whole series uploaded on youtube. I think people would at least skim through the videos so you'd get views.

62176601
These guys did it and got 5k views on average

https://m.youtube.com/@theLIVINproject/videos

62179536
>>62161086
If you're thinking about being influencer adjacent, why do you are about le workforce? You're a lost cause in either direction but to put them in the same line of choices is disgusting.

62179560
>>62170693
not for the heat death of the universe

62179939
>>62161076
the richest zoomer is a youtuber it got rich giving money away what an utterly gay generation

item.title: The LINK token has value — 49 replies — 6.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
The LINK token has value

Open thread • Replies: 49 • Updated: 2026-05-02 00:15 UTC

OP

The LINK token has value

Early replies (first 3)

62176216
New Chainlink report
https://bitwiseinvestments.com/crypto-market-insights/chainlink-in-plain-english

62176226
>>62176214
>In many cases, nodes must stake LINK before they can provide and be compensated for their services. If they provide incorrect data, they are penalized and lose a portion of the LINK they have staked. This system helps ensure the integrity of Chainlink’s services while also
reducing the free-floating supply of LINK.
Speaking in present tense. I assume this and the and the other clues we've seen in the past couple weeks mean staking imminent?

62176244
>>62176226
What other clues

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62176350
>>62176335
lol February

62176364
>>62176214
yeah about $5

62176404
>>62176214
An interesting read, but has bitwise ever released similar whitepapers for other tokens? They usually do quarterly reports but I'd be curious to see if they've been this bullish on other shit in the past. I still gotta read the paper and browse their site a lot more. Thank you for sharing OP

62176409
Holy shit I'm so fucking tired of waging, shut the fuck up and pump to $1000 already.

62176415
>>62176409
oh stinkie
who do you thinks gonna buy link at $10? $50? $100? $500? will it be losers like you? are you sure there is enough cash in all the losers of the world to pump the price to heckin a thousand USD?
face it stinkie: you lost the game of crypto, finance, and ultimately life itself

62176418
>>62176415
Don't reply to me ever again ranjeesh.

62176477
do stinkies even understand that pain and misery are LITERALLY their only friends

62176567
>saaars do not listen to the fuddergods, link token has value ssars! link token needed! do not redeem!

kek, who has ever even ehard of the bitwise

62176598
>>62176477
You’ve been posting here multiple times a day using the same formula for over a year. The style is played out. Aren’t you bored? Clearly you’re not very smart or inspired. You’re not dissuading the newfag or making anyone sell, so what are you doing? All you’re doing is showing the world how pathetic you are. Try something new, I promise it will feel good.

62176740
Is it march already? Weren't we supposed to be pumping in march???

62176756
I'm just going to hold until all tokens are distributed. Is that okay with you?

62176892
>>62176477
Welcome to another Chainlink thread
Be nice!

62176910
>>62176284
I dumped LINK and I moved into BTC. I started staking some recently and might start lending too with TBV now becoming more relevant. Will I make it with just BTC?

62176913
>>62176910
will you make it if what you're holding does a 2x?

62176989
>>62176914
What date is this video from?

62177088
>>62176295
Shartemis betray

62177113
>>62176284
>>62176298
>>62176344
>>62176415
>>62176477

62177436
>>62176989
2025. In the video they're expecting good things from 2026

62177765
>>62177436
I have expected good things for many years now. I'm still a believer but not a short term believer. Over the mid to long term I still think we'll win but it's definitely gonna be 10 years further down the line than we hoped for in 2018. In 2018 I'd said 2023 would be the year. But 3 years after that I don't see much in terms of price action, and who knows about actual adoption when the above video is still talking about trials, even if they are live trials.

62177825
>>62177765
at least they stopped calling them proof of concepts. bagholders really didn't like that term anymore. trials is also played out, so live trials it is. maybe they can call it "limited production usage" next, what do you think? would you like that term?

62177889
>>62176214
value like a new car has value, sure..

62177980
>>62176214
Only 273,000,000 LINK tokens left to dump before the team start dumping the reserve tokens. LOLOLOLOLOLOLOLOLOL. Get fucked stinkies

62177984
>>62177980
My ID is real fr no cap

62178008
>>62177825
Zach and that insufferable faggot, nullpackets, have been yapping about "live pre-production" for the past 2 years
it's truly hilarious

62178052
>>62178008
Ever since their breadcrumb boy at SWIFT stepped down there has been literally not a single shred of good news for the stinkers.

62178211
chainlink is the plumbing of the new finance world, and Link is the shit that flows through the pipes

on the other end of those pipes are link holders, whom sit with mouths open as receptacles to collect, drink, and shower in said shit

they do this with a smile on their face. why you ask? well, because, they are....

THE Cuckolds of crypto

62178228
just admit you lost the game of life, stinkies

62178833
>>62178052
>seething at Tom

Cripple detected

62179742
>>62177984
your id has fboy in it, that is really funny
>>62178228
thanks for posting in the Chainlink thread
>>62177088
How? There's nothing for them to betray, they posted speculation then left
>>62177765
>I have expected good things for many years now.
That's good, meaning the company has been running strong for years, meaning the it's not a scam, meaning the number is going up over time

item.title: Should I sleep with coworkers? — 29 replies — 4.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
Should I sleep with coworkers?

Open thread • Replies: 29 • Updated: 2026-05-02 00:41 UTC

OP

This IS business related, it impacts job prospects and my financial status

TLDR I've blown off three coworkers in 2026 so far and I'm wondering if I should stop being so uptight and create these psychosexual relationships with coworkers.

1) I feel like the drama wouldn't be worth it, and I could be negatively impacted since I'm a man
2) I work at a huge firm, the people I'd tap would have no overlap with my role, meaning they would have no incentive to rat me out in bad faith
3) I do feel like life would be more entertaining if I fucked around
4) I do want a long term relationship, a competent high earning parrner doesnt sound bad at all

Does anonymous of biz have any cautionary tales? Or any words of encouragement? I don't mind it if you just call me a retard, that's valuable input too.

Early replies (first 3)

62177168
>>62177163
No, don't do it. It can be extremely stressful. I fucked my way through nursing staff in my 20's and the kind of people who do this are usually.... Not the most mentally healthy or emotionally mature people.

62177170
>>62177163
id OovNkDoo, dog picture? Are you scooby's horndog cousin?
You shouldn't shit where you eat. Measuring their worth as a potential long term partner is another matter, though if they want to sleep with you right off the bat they're probably not a good candidate. I think you're right to avoid it.

62177177
>>62177163
if you're a wagie working some bullshit service industry job yeah whatever go for it. if you have a white collar career track job, don't do it. you'll probably get fired and the job market is horrendous.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62177190
didn't read tbqh but yes
you can always get a new job who gives a fuck

62177192
>>62177163
There are so many things that could go wrong.

62177198
youre gonna catch feelings and cry like a bitch in a year when she fucks around and cucks you HARD. joke of the floor you work on. stick to friend zoning them. you still get the validation knowing you can fuck them, and you actually cuck them by fucking other women in front of them. ( i mean chatting up and pulling these hoes when out with your female coworkers.) dont shit where you eat anon, that drama is what women live for, you have no idea, things you dont give a fuck about are everything to them...

62177201
>>62177163
>>62177177
People (used to) get married to coworkers at "white collar" jobs all the time.
In fact, work was the second most common place to meet your partner, for people out of school. Just out of school, and older.
Before everyone became a smartphone zombie, that is.

It's your job to judge whether the opposite side is worth it, or if they are out of their mind and have the brain of a moody teenager.

PS: The real logistical problem is actually working in an environment with enough women, who are worth the attention.

62177204
>>62177163
Yeah it'll fuck you over. The minute it helps one of these crazy bitches she'll claim assault. Or she'll blackmail you for it. Even if she doesn't you'll have to worry about it at one point or another. Not worth it.

62177213
i kind of remember what it was like to be this horny that this is how you think and what you think about constantly quite honestly it's fuckin hilarious that you're this screwed, literally enslaved by your own brain

62177215
>>62177177
I work in an office environment in finance, Im young and in a low level position, shouldve mentioned that.

>>62177190
I was thinking about this too, I dont think Ill have trouble finding my way around, plus I have savings

>>62177198
I'm not so worried about that... the drama part is scary, I dont trust women not to spill the beans on the office floor. But what is the worst that can happen?

62177224
I regret not fucking the older tall leggy blonde who wanted me to creampie her when I was 22 and working some bullshit low tier office job.
I am 41 now and still think about it often and regret not doing it.

62177239
>>62177213
Not much I can do about it pal. I am young. I ended a 4 year long relationship where we had great sex every day ~10 months ago. I cant wait for my sexdrive to go down but until then, what else can I do but enjoy the ride?

62177324
>>62177215
where else are you going to meet women? Everyone is always so 'busy' and in their own world. And fuck being a slave to your workspace. At worst you'll have a funny story about how you had to find a new job.

62177670
1 and 2 dont fit really, either its drama because you would see/work with them or its not

62177801
>>62177163
sure why not. imagine the whole world being the same company. would you still restrain yourself then? this is the same, just a bit smaller scale

62177823
>>62177163
Don't do it
If/when you get separate jobs, and/or new hires show up she's going to size them up in the exact same way she did to you. And (from her pov) if its good for her now, why can't it be better?
It'll feel like magic until the show stops and the lights come on

62177832
>>62177823
BTW
>t. Got married to a girl from my job, just to get accused of assault when she got 'involved' with a new job (that I regrettably set up for her).
Now divorced and sold to the state for feminist support slavery

62177917
>>62177163
I have fucked 4 of my staff, there are 2 more that I am probably going to as well but I am talking to someone outside of work for the first time in years.
No issues, no impact on my job or life. Just bee yourself (if you are an attractive man).

62177956
>>62177163
Why are you making up stories again Jamboy?

62178350
People say "don't shit where you eat" but that's advice from the age of lifetime employment to a single company. As long as you are solidly able to switch jobs if needed go for it. Don't fuck around with married women though.

62178359
What's it like knowing women want to have sex with you?

62178490
OP here, phoneposting

>>62177670
I suppose that's a point in favor of going for it... still, rumours are rumours, I dont want to be known as the guy who sleeps around with coworkers.

>>62177823
I am a pretty attractive and successful man, I dont think these women Im thinking of would want to leave me. I am sorry to hear you've been so brutally mistreated, hang in there.

>>62177917
>>62178350
Thanks mates

>>62178359
It is as with everything else. If you haven't got it, you crave it, if you have it you realise it is rather meaningless. Albeit I dont want to gaslight anyone into thinking that sex is not wonderful.

62179322
>women
He's talking about gay sex retards.
Yes, go and let them fuck you since your're already sucking them off.
This is the starting path into real wealth.

62179638
From my experience I would say it depends on your relative importance at work compared to hers.
I have regretted showing even small morsels of interest to female coworkers of similar title / status.
I have also regretted not chasing after girls with technician or similar titles

62179660
>>62177163
personally i don't do it, i wouldn't like having awkward situations at the office and i like my job

62179682
>>62177832
Sounds like ya picked wrong, what a cunt

>>62177163
Do you have good crazy-dar?
If so, go for it but be sure to be very up front about it either being a fling or long term.
If you fool a woman who wants long term, that could lead to problems even if she isn’t crazy. Definitely don’t sleep with multiple at once unless you’re on the same page about not being exclusive.

62179736
>>62177163

Never had a good experience doing it. If it doesn't lead to a relationship, they start going fucking crazy at you.

Better to just not dip your wick in the company ink.

62179804
>>62177163
don't shit where you eat, have you dealt with women before? Do you know how petty and fucked up they can be when things go bad?

62179808
>>62177163
don't be a pussy like 99% of the thread, fuck your coworkers every chance you get. there's nothing that can go wrong.
